front: Musical: this choreographer instructed dancers to snap their fingers to distinguish the Jets from the Sharks
Musical: alongside Robert Wise, this choreographer won the Best Director Oscar for the 1961 West Side Story film
Musical: in West Side Story's prologue, boys do ballet leaps after rising from bleachers where they had been snapping their fingers
Musical: in one number in West Side Story, dancers hunch over then spread their limbs wide to emulate a shaken soda bottle
Musical: Carol Lawrence premiered the role of Maria; this choreographer employed method acting techniques to foment animosity among the cast
Ballet: this 1944 ballet about three sailors on shore leave was inspired by Paul Cadmus's painting The Fleet's In!
Ballet: this ballet features a galop, waltz, and danzon by three solo dancers followed by a fistfight
Ballet: this first collaboration between Robbins and Bernstein was later adapted into the musical On the Town
Musical: this choreographer visited Orthodox Jewish weddings to get inspiration for the Bottle Dance in Fiddler on the Roof
Ballet: this comedic ballet features a 'mistake waltz' depicting daydreaming audience members at a Chopin recital
Ballet: this ballet uses Stravinsky's Concerto in D to depict female insects killing and devouring a male after sex
Ballet: this ballet ends with 'The Woman' yelling 'Stop!'; was banned for portraying a neurotic love triangle
Choreographer: this choreographer was the first ballet master of the New York City Ballet under Balanchine
Choreographer: this choreographer created Opus 19: The Dreamer for Mikhail Baryshnikov
Choreographer: this choreographer fell into the orchestra pit while yelling at the cast during rehearsals for Billion Dollar Baby
Choreographer: this choreographer was inspired by khon and piphat music for 'The Small House of Uncle Thomas' ballet within The King and I
Choreographer: this choreographer's 1953 Afternoon of a Faun duet is set in a ballet studio, inspired by Nijinsky's original
Choreographer: this choreographer was fond of Chopin's music, setting four ballets to it including In the Night (based on Nocturnes)
